a.name: moomz
a.tagline: Anonymous polls with live bars in 10 seconds

b.name: Typeform
b.tagline: Conversational forms and surveys with logic

title: moomz vs Typeform: which one for a fast poll?
description: moomz is a free anonymous poll built in 10 seconds with live realtime bars. Typeform is a polished survey builder with logic. See which fits.
intro: moomz and Typeform solve different problems. moomz is a one-screen vibe check you spin up in seconds and share via a short link. Typeform is a multi-step survey builder with branching logic, integrations, and brand styling.

highlights.label: Setup time
highlights.a: About 10 seconds: question, two to six options, share the link.
highlights.b: Several minutes for a real form: layout, logic, theme, settings.

highlights.label: Free plan
highlights.a: Free forever, no caps on questions or responses.
highlights.b: Free plan limited to 10 responses per month per form.

highlights.label: Account required
highlights.a: No signup. Just open moomz.com and ship.
highlights.b: Account required to build and publish any form.

highlights.label: Anonymous by default
highlights.a: Yes. No emails, no logins, cookie-based vote dedupe.
highlights.b: Forms collect responder data unless you disable fields and tracking.

highlights.label: Live results
highlights.a: Realtime bars update as votes land, no refresh.
highlights.b: Results dashboard updates after responses sync, no live bars.

highlights.label: Logic and branching
highlights.a: Single-question vibe check. No conditional paths.
highlights.b: Full logic jumps, calculations, hidden fields, variables.

highlights.label: Integrations
highlights.a: Share link works in any chat or social app. No native integrations.
highlights.b: Hundreds of integrations: Slack, HubSpot, Notion, Zapier, webhooks.

highlights.label: Mobile experience
highlights.a: Mobile-first dark UI, swipe feed, OG previews when shared.
highlights.b: Responsive forms that adapt cleanly to phones.

verdict: If you want a fast anonymous vote from friends, a class, or a group chat, moomz wins on speed and zero friction. If you need branching, lead capture, or formal data collection, Typeform is the better tool.
pickA: Pick moomz when you want a vibe check, a quick decision, or a viral poll your audience can answer in two taps without signing in.
pickB: Pick Typeform when you need a multi-question survey with logic, file uploads, payments, or CRM integrations.

faq.q: Is moomz really free?
faq.a: Yes. Polls, votes, live results, and short links are free forever with no response caps.

faq.q: Do voters need an account?
faq.a: No. moomz never asks voters to sign in. Typeform respondents do not need an account either, but the creator does.

faq.q: Can I see who voted on moomz?
faq.a: No. Voting is anonymous by design. moomz uses a cookie to stop the same browser from voting twice, but identities are never stored.

faq.q: Does Typeform have a free plan?
faq.a: Yes, but it caps responses at 10 per month per form. moomz has no such cap.

faq.q: Can moomz replace Typeform for a real survey?
faq.a: No. moomz is a single-question vibe check. If you need branching, file uploads, or 10-question flows, use Typeform.

faq.q: How fast can I publish a poll on moomz?
faq.a: Around 10 seconds. Type the question, add 2 to 6 options, hit publish, copy the link.

a.name: moomz
a.tagline: Anonymous polls with live bars in 10 seconds

b.name: SurveyMonkey
b.tagline: Enterprise survey platform with question banks and analytics

title: moomz vs SurveyMonkey: poll or full survey?
description: moomz is a free anonymous poll with live realtime bars. SurveyMonkey is an enterprise survey platform with deep analytics. Compare both.
intro: moomz is built for quick anonymous reactions you can share to a group chat. SurveyMonkey is built for structured research, employee feedback, and customer satisfaction work with detailed reporting.

highlights.label: Time to ship
highlights.a: About 10 seconds for a public poll with a shareable link.
highlights.b: 5 to 15 minutes minimum for a basic survey with proper settings.

highlights.label: Pricing
highlights.a: Free forever, no response cap.
highlights.b: Free tier limited to 10 questions and 40 responses per survey.

highlights.label: Setup friction
highlights.a: No signup, no email, no template picking.
highlights.b: Account, template selection, sharing channel setup.

highlights.label: Live results
highlights.a: Vote bars update in realtime as people answer.
highlights.b: Reports refresh after responses sync. No live spectator view.

highlights.label: Anonymous voting
highlights.a: Anonymous by default, cookie dedupe, no IP logging displayed to creator.
highlights.b: Anonymous mode exists but must be configured per survey.

highlights.label: Question types
highlights.a: Single multi-choice question, 2 to 6 options.
highlights.b: Dozens: matrix, ranking, NPS, sliders, file upload, logic jumps.

highlights.label: Analytics depth
highlights.a: Live counts and percentages. No cross-tab analysis.
highlights.b: Cross-tabulation, filtering, segmentation, SPSS export.

highlights.label: Team and enterprise features
highlights.a: Solo tool, no workspaces, no roles.
highlights.b: Workspaces, SSO, audit logs, HIPAA tier on Enterprise plan.

verdict: moomz crushes SurveyMonkey on speed, price, and viral sharing for casual polls. SurveyMonkey is the right pick when you need structured research, team workflows, or enterprise compliance.
pickA: Pick moomz for a fast public vibe check, a group decision, or a poll you want to drop in a group chat without anyone signing in.
pickB: Pick SurveyMonkey for HR engagement surveys, customer satisfaction tracking, market research, or anything that needs cross-tab analysis.

faq.q: Can I export moomz results to a spreadsheet?
faq.a: Not natively today. SurveyMonkey exports to CSV, XLS, SPSS, and PDF on paid plans.

faq.q: Is SurveyMonkey free?
faq.a: It has a limited free plan capped at 10 questions and 40 responses per survey. moomz has no such limits.

faq.q: Which is better for a school class?
faq.a: moomz, because students do not need to sign in and the live bar reveals are part of the fun.

faq.q: Can I run a long survey on moomz?
faq.a: No. moomz is one question with 2 to 6 options. For multi-question flows pick SurveyMonkey.

faq.q: Does moomz track who answered?
faq.a: No. Voting is anonymous. Only a cookie is set to prevent the same browser from voting twice.

faq.q: Can I add my brand colors?
faq.a: moomz uses a fixed dark glass aesthetic. SurveyMonkey paid plans allow custom themes and logos.

a.name: moomz
a.tagline: Anonymous polls with live bars in 10 seconds

b.name: JotForm
b.tagline: Drag-and-drop form builder with payments and PDF

title: moomz vs JotForm: poll or full form?
description: moomz is a free anonymous poll built in 10 seconds with live bars. JotForm is a powerful drag-and-drop form builder. Compare them side by side.
intro: moomz exists to ask one question fast and watch the answers stream in live. JotForm exists to build any kind of form, from registration to payment to incident reports, with files, signatures, and PDFs attached.

highlights.label: Speed
highlights.a: Question to link in about 10 seconds, no template needed.
highlights.b: Drag-and-drop builder takes a few minutes even for simple forms.

highlights.label: Free tier
highlights.a: Free forever, unlimited polls and votes.
highlights.b: Free plan capped at 5 forms and 100 monthly submissions.

highlights.label: No account
highlights.a: Skip signup, just go.
highlights.b: Account required to build, share, and receive submissions.

highlights.label: Anonymous voting
highlights.a: Anonymous by default, no email, no IP shown.
highlights.b: Forms can be anonymous but the default collects metadata.

highlights.label: Realtime results
highlights.a: Live bars animate as votes land.
highlights.b: Submissions appear in the inbox after the responder hits send.

highlights.label: Form richness
highlights.a: Single question, 2 to 6 options, optional image.
highlights.b: 10000-plus templates: payments, signatures, file uploads, conditional logic.

highlights.label: Integrations
highlights.a: Just the share link, works anywhere.
highlights.b: 150-plus integrations: Stripe, PayPal, Google Sheets, Salesforce, Slack.

highlights.label: PDF and storage
highlights.a: No file capture or PDF generation.
highlights.b: Auto-generate PDFs from submissions, attach uploaded files.

verdict: moomz wins on raw speed and zero friction for a one-shot vote. JotForm wins the moment you need files, payments, signatures, or any multi-field form. Choose by use case, not by feature count.
pickA: Pick moomz when you want a public vibe check that takes 10 seconds to publish and runs without anyone signing in.
pickB: Pick JotForm when you need a registration form, payment form, signed waiver, or any structured intake with multiple fields.

faq.q: Can JotForm do live polls?
faq.a: Not natively. You can build a single-question form, but you won't get live animated bars or a public results page.

faq.q: Does moomz support payments?
faq.a: No. moomz is a free poll tool. For paid registration or product orders, use JotForm.

faq.q: Which is better for a quick group vote?
faq.a: moomz, because there is no signup and the link opens directly into the vote.

faq.q: Is JotForm free?
faq.a: It has a free plan with 5 forms and 100 monthly submissions. moomz has no such caps.

faq.q: Can I collect files with moomz?
faq.a: No. moomz only collects votes on a multi-choice question. JotForm handles uploads, signatures, and PDFs.

faq.q: Do moomz polls expire?
faq.a: Polls stay live indefinitely. JotForm forms can be auto-closed by date or submission count.

a.name: moomz
a.tagline: Anonymous polls with live bars in 10 seconds

b.name: Kahoot
b.tagline: Game-based quizzes for classrooms and training

title: moomz vs Kahoot: vibe check or game quiz?
description: moomz is a free anonymous poll with live realtime bars. Kahoot is a gamified quiz platform for classrooms and training. See which fits.
intro: moomz is a quick anonymous poll you share in a group chat. Kahoot is a game-show style quiz built for live classrooms and training, with PINs, leaderboards, music, and timers.

highlights.label: Async play
highlights.a: Polls are async: people vote whenever they open the link.
highlights.b: Live games require everyone joined at the same time with a PIN.

highlights.label: Free plan
highlights.a: Free forever for creators and voters.
highlights.b: Free tier capped at 40 players per game and limited question types.

highlights.label: Setup time
highlights.a: 10 seconds from idea to shareable link.
highlights.b: 5 to 20 minutes to build a multi-question quiz.

highlights.label: Anonymity
highlights.a: Voters are fully anonymous, no nickname needed.
highlights.b: Players must pick a nickname, which appears on the leaderboard.

highlights.label: Shareability
highlights.a: Short link works in any chat or feed.
highlights.b: Needs a PIN entered on kahoot.it. No deep link to a game in progress.

highlights.label: Gamification
highlights.a: Streaks and achievement badges, but no quiz scoring.
highlights.b: Points by speed, leaderboards, music, podiums, team mode.

highlights.label: Classroom fit
highlights.a: Great for a class vibe check, not a graded quiz.
highlights.b: Built for live lessons with reports, assignments, and LMS integrations.

highlights.label: Question variety
highlights.a: One multi-choice question per poll.
highlights.b: Multi-question quizzes: true-false, type-answer, puzzle, slider.

verdict: moomz wins for casual async polls you want to drop in any chat. Kahoot wins for live classroom quizzes with scoring, leaderboards, and a host driving the session.
pickA: Pick moomz when you want a quick group decision, a class warm-up vote, or a viral poll that anyone can answer without joining a session.
pickB: Pick Kahoot when you are running a synchronous quiz, training session, or trivia night with a live host and a scoreboard.

faq.q: Can Kahoot run async?
faq.a: Yes, via Challenge mode, but it still requires a Kahoot account and a multi-question quiz. moomz is async out of the box.

faq.q: Does moomz have leaderboards?
faq.a: No. moomz tracks streaks and points for the voter, but there is no per-poll leaderboard. Kahoot is the leaderboard tool.

faq.q: Which is better for a classroom poll?
faq.a: moomz for a quick anonymous vibe check. Kahoot for a graded quiz with scoring.

faq.q: Do voters need an app?
faq.a: No app for either. moomz opens any URL. Kahoot uses kahoot.it with a PIN.

faq.q: Can I ask multiple questions on moomz?
faq.a: Not in one poll. Create separate polls and share each link. Kahoot stacks questions into one quiz.

faq.q: Is Kahoot free for teachers?
faq.a: Basic Kahoot is free but limited. Paid plans unlock more players and question types. moomz is free with no caps.

a.name: moomz
a.tagline: Anonymous polls with live bars in 10 seconds

b.name: AhaSlides
b.tagline: Interactive slides and live polls for presenters

title: moomz vs AhaSlides: standalone poll or live slides?
description: moomz is a free anonymous poll with live realtime bars, shareable as a standalone link. AhaSlides embeds polls inside live slides. Compare them.
intro: moomz is a standalone poll link you share to a chat or feed. AhaSlides is a presentation tool that mixes slides, polls, quizzes, and word clouds for live audiences.

highlights.label: Async vs live
highlights.a: Async by default: people vote whenever they open the link.
highlights.b: Designed for live sessions where a presenter drives the slides.

highlights.label: Pricing
highlights.a: Free forever, no audience cap.
highlights.b: Free plan capped at 50 audience members per event.

highlights.label: Time to publish
highlights.a: About 10 seconds from idea to shareable link.
highlights.b: Several minutes to build a deck with poll slides inserted.

highlights.label: No account
highlights.a: Creators and voters both go through with zero signup.
highlights.b: Account required for presenters.

highlights.label: Shareability outside a session
highlights.a: Lives at a moomz.com short link, social previews built in.
highlights.b: Polls live inside a presentation, harder to share standalone.

highlights.label: Visual variety
highlights.a: One question screen with bars.
highlights.b: Polls, quizzes, word clouds, brainstorming, Q and A, scales, ranking.

highlights.label: Presenter mode
highlights.a: No presenter view, no big-screen mode.
highlights.b: Full presenter console with slides, results, and Q and A moderation.

highlights.label: Reporting
highlights.a: Live counts only, no after-event report.
highlights.b: Post-event report with per-slide breakdown and CSV export.

verdict: moomz is the right pick when you just want to share a poll link. AhaSlides is the right pick when you are presenting live and want polls baked into the slide deck.
pickA: Pick moomz when you want a standalone vote that lives forever at its own link, with no presenter required.
pickB: Pick AhaSlides when you are giving a talk, training, or workshop and need polls inside your presentation.

faq.q: Can AhaSlides do standalone polls?
faq.a: Yes, but the link opens inside the AhaSlides player, not a public poll page like moomz.

faq.q: Is AhaSlides free for large audiences?
faq.a: The free tier caps at 50 audience members per event. moomz has no audience cap.

faq.q: Which is better for a conference talk?
faq.a: AhaSlides, because polls sit inside your deck. moomz works as a separate link in the chat.

faq.q: Can I show moomz on a big screen?
faq.a: Yes, just open the poll URL in a browser. The live bars update in realtime as votes come in.

faq.q: Does moomz support word clouds?
faq.a: No. moomz is multi-choice only. AhaSlides offers word clouds and free-text questions.

faq.q: Can I quiz an audience with moomz?
faq.a: Not as a scored quiz. moomz is a vote, not a right-or-wrong quiz. Use AhaSlides or Kahoot for scoring.

a.name: moomz
a.tagline: Anonymous polls with live bars in 10 seconds

b.name: Vevox
b.tagline: Audience interaction for meetings, lectures, and town halls

title: moomz vs Vevox: free poll or enterprise audience tool?
description: moomz is a free anonymous poll with realtime bars and a public link. Vevox is an enterprise audience polling and Q and A tool. Compare both.
intro: moomz is a free, public, link-first poll. Vevox is a paid enterprise platform built for large meetings, lectures, and town halls with Q and A moderation, integrations, and detailed reporting.

highlights.label: Cost
highlights.a: Free forever for creators and voters.
highlights.b: Free tier exists but is limited. Real use requires a paid plan.

highlights.label: Time to publish
highlights.a: 10 seconds, no signup.
highlights.b: Account, session setup, branding, then sharing.

highlights.label: Public reach
highlights.a: Shareable short link anyone on the web can open.
highlights.b: Sessions are typically scoped to attendees of a meeting.

highlights.label: Anonymous voting
highlights.a: Anonymous by default with cookie dedupe.
highlights.b: Anonymous mode is supported but session-scoped.

highlights.label: Mobile UX
highlights.a: Mobile-first, dark glass, swipe-friendly feed.
highlights.b: Mobile web app, clean but tuned for in-meeting use.

highlights.label: Q and A moderation
highlights.a: No threaded Q and A or upvotes.
highlights.b: Full Q and A with upvotes, moderation, anonymous mode.

highlights.label: Enterprise integrations
highlights.a: None. Share-link only.
highlights.b: Microsoft Teams, Zoom, PowerPoint, Webex add-ins.

highlights.label: Reporting and exports
highlights.a: Live counts only.
highlights.b: Per-session reports, attendance, CSV and XLSX export.

verdict: moomz is the better choice when you want a public poll anyone can answer in two taps. Vevox is the better choice for serious enterprise audience interaction inside Teams, Zoom, or PowerPoint.
pickA: Pick moomz for a fast public vote you can drop in any chat or feed, no signup, no enterprise plan needed.
pickB: Pick Vevox when you are running an internal town hall, lecture, or all-hands inside Teams, Zoom, or PowerPoint with Q and A moderation.

faq.q: Is Vevox free?
faq.a: It has a limited free tier. Most real use requires a paid subscription. moomz is free with no caps.

faq.q: Can moomz integrate with Teams or Zoom?
faq.a: Not natively. You paste the moomz link into the chat. Vevox has native add-ins for Teams, Zoom, and PowerPoint.

faq.q: Which is better for a town hall?
faq.a: Vevox, because of Q and A moderation and meeting-tool integrations. moomz is better for a quick public pulse.

faq.q: Does moomz support upvoted questions?
faq.a: No. moomz is a vote, not a Q and A. Vevox handles Q and A with upvotes.

faq.q: Can I see who voted on moomz?
faq.a: No. Voting is anonymous, with only a cookie used to stop double votes.

faq.q: Can Vevox run async?
faq.a: Sessions can stay open for a window, but the workflow assumes a live event. moomz is async by default.

a.name: moomz
a.tagline: Anonymous polls with live bars in 10 seconds

title: moomz vs Instagram Story Poll: which is better?
description: moomz is a free anonymous poll with up to 6 options and live realtime bars. Instagram Story polls cap at 2 options for 24h. Compare them.
intro: Both let you ask a quick question. moomz is a standalone link with up to 6 options and live results that stay forever. Instagram Story polls live inside a 24-hour story and cap at two options.

highlights.label: Option count
highlights.a: 2 to 6 options per poll.
highlights.b: Exactly 2 options on the basic poll sticker.

highlights.label: Lifetime
highlights.a: Polls live forever at a permanent moomz.com link.
highlights.b: Story polls disappear after 24 hours.

highlights.label: Anonymity for voters
highlights.a: Voters are fully anonymous.
highlights.b: Instagram shows the creator exactly who voted for what.

highlights.label: Audience
highlights.a: Anyone with the link can vote, on or off social.
highlights.b: Only your Instagram followers (or public account viewers) can see and vote.

highlights.label: Where it lives
highlights.a: Standalone page with OG preview, shareable in any chat or feed.
highlights.b: Locked inside the Instagram Stories tab.

highlights.label: In-app distribution
highlights.a: Needs a link drop in DMs, chats, or other apps.
highlights.b: Auto-served to followers in the Stories feed.

highlights.label: Speed to publish
highlights.a: About 10 seconds in the browser.
highlights.b: Also about 10 seconds inside the Instagram app.

highlights.label: Image support
highlights.a: Optional image attached to the poll itself.
highlights.b: Image is the story background, not part of the poll data.

verdict: moomz wins for permanent, anonymous, multi-option polls anyone can vote on. Instagram Story polls win when you only need a quick 2-option vote inside your existing follower base.
pickA: Pick moomz when you want more than 2 options, true anonymity for voters, or a poll that lives forever at its own link.
pickB: Pick the Instagram Story poll for a 24-hour two-option vote in front of your followers without leaving the app.

faq.q: Can Instagram polls have more than 2 options?
faq.a: The basic poll sticker is 2 options. The emoji slider gives a 1D scale, but multi-option voting with bars is not native.

faq.q: Are Instagram poll votes anonymous?
faq.a: No. The creator sees every voter's username and pick. moomz votes are fully anonymous.

faq.q: Do moomz polls expire?
faq.a: No. moomz polls stay live forever. Instagram Story polls expire after 24 hours.

faq.q: Can I share a moomz poll on Instagram?
faq.a: Yes. Drop the moomz.com short link in your bio, story link sticker, or DMs.

faq.q: Does moomz need an account?
faq.a: No. You can create and share a poll without signing up. Instagram needs an account to post a story.

faq.q: Which gets more votes?
faq.a: Instagram leverages your follower graph. moomz spreads through any chat or link drop and is more shareable to non-Instagram users.

a.name: moomz
a.tagline: Anonymous polls with live bars in 10 seconds

b.name: WhatsApp Poll
b.tagline: In-chat polls visible to a group's members

title: moomz vs WhatsApp Poll: which one to use?
description: moomz is a free anonymous poll with live realtime bars and a permanent public link. WhatsApp polls stay inside one chat. Compare both.
intro: Both let you put a question in front of a group fast. WhatsApp polls only work inside one chat and expose every voter's name. moomz polls live at a public link, stay anonymous, and reach anyone you send the URL to.

highlights.label: Anonymity
highlights.a: Anonymous by default for every voter.
highlights.b: Everyone in the chat sees who voted for what.

highlights.label: Reach
highlights.a: Anyone with the link can vote, on any platform.
highlights.b: Only members of the chat where the poll was posted.

highlights.label: Permanence
highlights.a: Lives forever at a moomz.com link with social preview.
highlights.b: Buried in chat history once new messages roll in.

highlights.label: Cross-platform
highlights.a: Works in any chat app, web, social feed.
highlights.b: Locked to WhatsApp. Cannot be answered from another app.

highlights.label: Image support
highlights.a: Optional image attached to the poll itself.
highlights.b: No image inside the poll card.

highlights.label: In-chat convenience
highlights.a: Needs a link drop. One extra tap to open.
highlights.b: Native to the chat, no link to open, results inline.

highlights.label: Speed to publish
highlights.a: Around 10 seconds in the browser.
highlights.b: Around 10 seconds inside WhatsApp.

highlights.label: Option count
highlights.a: 2 to 6 options.
highlights.b: Up to 12 options, but no live animated bars.

verdict: moomz is the better pick when you want anonymity, cross-platform reach, or a poll that lives forever. WhatsApp polls are the better pick when the audience is one chat and you do not care that names are visible.
pickA: Pick moomz when you want anonymous voting, a permanent link to share anywhere, or live animated bars on a public results page.
pickB: Pick WhatsApp polls when the question only matters to one specific group chat and you want zero extra clicks.

faq.q: Are WhatsApp polls anonymous?
faq.a: No. Every member of the chat can see who voted for what. moomz is fully anonymous.

faq.q: Can I share a moomz poll on WhatsApp?
faq.a: Yes. Copy the moomz.com short link and paste it in any chat. The OG preview renders inline.

faq.q: Do WhatsApp polls work on Web or Desktop?
faq.a: Yes, inside WhatsApp. They cannot be answered by anyone outside that chat. moomz works in any browser.

faq.q: Can I edit a poll after sending?
faq.a: WhatsApp polls cannot be edited. moomz polls also cannot be edited but stay live indefinitely so you can keep collecting votes.

faq.q: How many options does WhatsApp allow?
faq.a: Up to 12 options per poll, without live animated bars. moomz caps at 6 options but renders realtime bars.

faq.q: Which is more viral?
faq.a: moomz, because the link can leave the original chat and spread across platforms. WhatsApp polls die inside the chat they were posted to.
